data.title: Olam plans to allocate $2B from Saudi sale to reduce debt
China suspends rare earth exports as trade war with U.S. escalates - report
data.description:
China has suspended rare earth exports in retaliation to President Trump’s recent tariff hikes, The New York Times reported.

The Chinese government is drafting a new regulatory system for exports, and while the policies are being framed, the shipments of the magnets, essential for the manufacturing of almost everything ranging from cars to missiles, have been halted at many Chinese ports, according to the report.

On April 4, China imposed new restrictions on the export of six heavy rare earth elements—metals that are refined exclusively in China—as well as rare earth magnets.

Beijing produces around 90% of the world's rare earths, a group of 17 elements used across the defense, electric vehicle, energy and electronics industries.

Meanwhile, China's exports of rare earths rose 20.31% in March from a year earlier as overseas consumers booked more cargoes, amid supply disruptions from the ongoing conflict in major supplier Myanmar.

China's exports of the group of 17 minerals stood at 5,666.3 metric tons last month, Reuters reported, citing data from the General Administration of Customs.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Torchlight Innovations and Innovation Mining reach definitive pact for proposed merger
data.description:
Torchlight Innovations (TLX.P:CA) has announced a definitive agreement to merge with Innovation Mining Inc., a clean-tech firm developing a non-toxic gold extraction technology called RZOLV.

The deal, structured as a "three-cornered amalgamation," positions Torchlight to exit its capital pool company status and acquire all the issued and outstanding securities of Innovation.

Under the "three-cornered amalgamation," Torchlight will merge its wholly-owned subsidiary (Subco) with Innovation Mining, creating a new entity (Amalco) that becomes Torchlight’s wholly-owned subsidiary. 

Upon completion of the merger, the resulting issuer will take over the business of Innovation as a Tier 2 technology Issuer.

In connection with the deal, Torchlight intends to consolidate its common shares on a 1.93:1 basis and change its name to "Innovation Mining" or such other name as may be determined by Innovation, the company added.

Following consolidation, Innovation Mining's owners will receive 55,011,000 shares of the new business in return for their holdings, as well as a slew of warrants and options.

The deal is supported by two finance components: SR Financing: A $2 million non-brokered private placement of subscription receipts convertible into shares and warrants after the transaction; and PP Financing: A $752,500 private placement of units (shares and warrants) priced at $0.35 pre-split ($0.28 post-split).

data.title: Seahawk Gold is now Seahawk Ventures
Bank of Japan chief Ueda warns increased uncertainty from U.S. tariff
Gold sprints to fresh record high, Goldman Sachs boosts end-2025 forecast to $3,700/oz
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs has increased its end-2025 gold price forecast to $3,700 per ounce from $3,300, its third such hike this year, as trade tensions escalate. 
  • Bullion hit a record high of $3,245.42 earlier in the day. Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) was down 0.2% to $3,230.85 an ounce, as of 0206 am ET.
  • The Bank anticipates stronger-than-expected central bank demand and ETF inflows. 
  • The investment bank had in March hiked its 2025 gold price target to $3,300/oz. 
  • Goldman Sachs warned that in an extreme risk case, gold could reach as high as $4,500/oz by end-2025, according to reports.
  • Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) prices  marched higher on Friday, marking a significant move for the precious metal as it clocked its strongest three-day performance since March 2020—when markets were reeling from the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Trump says he does not want a foreign company to control US Steel
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Sunday reiterated his opposition to the sale of U.S. Steel (NYSE:X) to a foreign company. 

The statement reinforces his earlier remarks that cast doubt on the proposed $14 billion acquisition of the American steelmaker by Japan's Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY).

Trump made the comments while traveling to Washington on Air Force One.

Last Wednesday, the President's comment that he didn't want to see US Steel “go to Japan” caused a 7% drop in the company's shares. Following this, both companies stated they were collaborating with the administration “to secure a significant investment.”

Trump previously opposed Nippon's (OTCPK:NPSCY) proposed takeover of U.S. Steel (NYSE:X), but has said that he'd be open to the Japanese company buying a minority stake instead.

The original deal, for Nippon to buy U.S. Steel, which was announced in December 2023, faced political headwinds from the start, with both Trump and Biden vowing to kill the transaction.

data.title: KKR appoints former U.S. General David Petraeus as Middle East business head
Ennis acquires Northeastern Envelope to expand manufacturing capabilities
data.description:
Ennis (NYSE:EBF) has announced the acquisition of Northeastern Envelope, a Pennsylvania-based envelope manufacturer.

The financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

Northeastern Envelope, known for its custom converting and manufacturing capabilities, maintains an extensive inventory of single and double window envelopes with same-day shipping capabilities.

"The addition of Northeastern to our existing facilities Wisco Envelopes and National Imprint strengthens Ennis’ envelope converting and printing in this strategic part of the country where we service many customers," said Keith Walters, Chairman, President & CEO of Ennis, Inc.
